context_comparison.clock_assumptions: GNSS time and receiver clock solved by GNSS
context_comparison.reported_or_modeled_accuracy: meter-level context from GPS.gov under usable GNSS; not modeled here as a final value

context_comparison.fair_comparison_caveat: aided baseline with external data path

context_comparison.infrastructure_required: GNSS plus correction service, base station, CORS, or precise products.
context_comparison.reported_or_modeled_accuracy: centimeter-class context under good correction-service conditions

context_comparison.clock_assumptions: paper-dependent satellite and receiver clock/orbit treatment
context_comparison.fair_comparison_caveat: literature context only unless assumptions are reproduced
context_comparison.infrastructure_required: LEO signals, orbit/clock knowledge or estimation, signal-specific receiver.
context_comparison.reported_or_modeled_accuracy: literature examples from meters to tens of meters depending on aiding

context_comparison.clock_assumptions: GNSS prior may aid position or clocks
context_comparison.infrastructure_required: brief or periodic GNSS plus NTN measurements.
context_comparison.reported_or_modeled_accuracy: scenario sweep in this branch, non-final

context_comparison.clock_assumptions: joint position/clock estimation relative to reference satellite
context_comparison.fair_comparison_caveat: main manuscript concept, but this branch is diagnostic only
context_comparison.gnss_access_required: no, unless using an aided variant
context_comparison.infrastructure_required: cooperative terrestrial/non-terrestrial measurements in the manuscript model.
context_comparison.reported_or_modeled_accuracy: existing Stage B/C7 diagnostics reused; not final manuscript-ready

degraded_gnss_rows.basis: GPS.gov smartphone open-sky context, not a final manuscript value

executive_summary: This branch implements non-final GPS/GNSS-style baseline taxonomy, literature context, position-prior sweeps, clock-prior sweeps, and intermittent GNSS holdover diagnostics.

literature.comparison_caveat: User accuracy depends on geometry, blockage, atmosphere, multipath, and receiver quality; URE is not user position accuracy.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: Open-sky GNSS, broadcast GNSS signals, GNSS control segment, receiver solution for position and time.
literature.label: standalone_gnss_reference
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: Smartphone open-sky example 4.9 m radius; high-quality single-frequency FAA data <=1.82 m horizontal, 95%; GPS URE <=2.0 m, 95%.
literature.source_title: GPS.gov: GPS Accuracy

literature.comparison_caveat: Service commitment/global metric, not a particular UE, urban, or NTN receiver result.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: Civil GPS SPS, healthy constellation, global service definitions, GPS control segment.
literature.label: standalone_gnss_reference
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: 2024 SPS metrics include <=8 m horizontal 95% global average, <=13 m vertical 95% global average, and <=30 ns time transfer 95%.
literature.source_title: GPS.gov: GPS Performance

literature.comparison_caveat: Not a generic mobile UE clock guarantee.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: Specialized time-transfer receiver, fixed site, GNSS time infrastructure.
literature.label: standalone_gnss_reference
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: GPS time-transfer accuracy relative to UTC(USNO) <=30 ns, 95%, for specialized fixed time-transfer receivers.
literature.source_title: GPS.gov: GPS Accuracy - timing

literature.comparison_caveat: Standards support and architecture, not an accuracy guarantee.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: NG-RAN and LMF positioning architecture.
literature.label: gnss_aided_initialization
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: NG-RAN positioning supports A-GNSS along with NR positioning methods such as DL-TDOA, DL-AoD, Multi-RTT, UL-TDOA, and UL-AoA.
literature.source_title: 3GPP TS 38.305 UE positioning in NG-RAN

literature.comparison_caveat: Protocol support is not a standalone accuracy claim.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: Location server such as E-SMLC, LMF, or SLP; cellular control/user-plane assistance.
literature.label: gnss_aided_initialization
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: LPP covers E-UTRA and NR positioning and includes A-GNSS assistance/provide-location procedures.
literature.source_title: 3GPP TS 37.355 LTE Positioning Protocol (LPP)

literature.comparison_caveat: Defines assistance architecture/test cases, not a universal accuracy number.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: SUPL-enabled terminal, SUPL location platform/server, IP/user-plane connectivity.
literature.label: gnss_aided_initialization
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: SUPL test cases include A-GPS/A-GNSS SET-assisted and SET-based modes.
literature.source_title: OMA SUPL v2.0 enabler test specification

literature.comparison_caveat: Aviation augmentation infrastructure; not unaided GNSS or GNSS-denied JCLS.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: WAAS reference stations, master stations, GEO broadcast, and aviation receiver modes.
literature.label: gnss_correction_service_reference
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: FAA monitored WAAS reports provide site-level horizontal/vertical 95% performance statistics under aviation integrity modes.
literature.source_title: FAA WAAS Performance Analysis Report

literature.comparison_caveat: Precision and absolute accuracy differ; requires correction infrastructure and favorable GNSS conditions.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: Known base station, rover, correction data link, calibration, and field quality control.
literature.label: gnss_correction_service_reference
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: Survey-grade real-time precision often cited around 1 cm + 1 ppm horizontal and 2 cm + 1 ppm vertical at 1 sigma, when procedures and ambiguity fixing are successful.
literature.source_title: NOAA NGS User Guidelines for Single Base Real Time GNSS Positioning

literature.comparison_caveat: Network correction infrastructure is not comparable to GNSS-denied cooperative JCLS.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: CORS/RTN network, datum alignment, cellular/internet data link, and user QC.
literature.label: gnss_correction_service_reference
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: RTN guidelines discuss centimeter-class horizontal/vertical precision under controlled network and field procedures.
literature.source_title: NOAA NGS Guidelines for Real Time GNSS Networks

literature.comparison_caveat: Postprocessed survey infrastructure, not real-time standalone GNSS or JCLS.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: Reference-station network, code/carrier data, postprocessing, and NSRS alignment.
literature.label: gnss_correction_service_reference
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: CORS supports postprocessed high-accuracy positioning workflows through fiducial reference stations.
literature.source_title: NOAA CORS Network

literature.comparison_caveat: High-infrastructure reference, not a fair GNSS-denied baseline.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: Reference stations, correction streams, satellite/internet delivery, or commercial correction service.
literature.label: gnss_correction_service_reference
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: RTK, DGNSS, SBAS, and PPP are correction methods; RTK uses correction links, PPP can provide centimetre-level accuracy with global corrections.
literature.source_title: NovAtel: RTK vs PPP correction services

literature.comparison_caveat: Correction products are external infrastructure and may have latency or service constraints.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: Global GNSS monitoring and analysis-center infrastructure.
literature.label: gnss_correction_service_reference
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: IGS provides precise orbit and clock products; PPP-AR uses precise products and bias information.
literature.source_title: International GNSS Service products / PPP-AR

literature.comparison_caveat: Correction-stream performance is not direct user position accuracy and assumes external infrastructure.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: Real-time global tracking, analysis centers, correction streams, and PPP receiver processing.
literature.label: gnss_correction_service_reference
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: IGS real-time correction literature reports centimeter-class orbit and sub-nanosecond clock correction targets/behavior for PPP inputs.
literature.source_title: Performance of real-time IGS satellite clocks for PPP

literature.comparison_caveat: Literature context only; assumptions differ from JCLS cooperative NTN.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: Signal-specific receiver, TLE/orbit information, batching/EKF, sometimes altitude or other aiding.
literature.label: leo_pnt_literature_reference
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: Survey reports examples including 7.7 m 2D Starlink with altitude aiding, 25.9 m 2D and 33.5 m 3D without external altitude over 800 s using six satellites.
literature.source_title: Receiver architectures for positioning with low earth orbit satellite signals: a survey

literature.comparison_caveat: Proof-of-concept experiment, not an operational PNT service or infrastructure-matched JCLS baseline.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: Passive/non-cooperative Starlink signal exploitation, carrier-phase tracking, static receiver, and aiding for best case.
literature.label: leo_pnt_literature_reference
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: Reported experimental examples include 7.7 m 2D error with known altitude and tens-of-meters 2D/3D errors without external altitude over an extended batch.
literature.source_title: The First Carrier Phase Tracking and Positioning Results With Starlink LEO Satellite Signals

literature.comparison_caveat: Observability context, not a general accuracy benchmark.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: Known LEO satellite state over time and pseudorange extraction from LEO signals.
literature.label: leo_pnt_literature_reference
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: Analyzes observability from LEO pseudorange measurements over time and includes experimental demonstrations.
literature.source_title: Observability Analysis of Opportunistic Receiver Localization with LEO Satellite Pseudorange Measurements

literature.comparison_caveat: Design-study context, not a reproduced baseline for this manuscript.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: NR-NTN physical-layer enhancements, PRS resource design, LEO orbit/signal simulation framework.
literature.label: leo_pnt_literature_reference
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: Discusses LEO-based NR-NTN positioning as complementary infrastructure to GNSS and potentially an alternative with enhancements.
literature.source_title: LEO-based Positioning: Foundations, Signal Design, and Receiver Enhancements for 6G NTN

literature.comparison_caveat: Useful for framing weak/unavailable GNSS, not for direct numerical comparison.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: 3GPP-related NTN positioning assumptions and CRLB simulation study.
literature.label: leo_pnt_literature_reference
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: Identifies multi-LEO positioning and GNSS-augmented LEO positioning when insufficient GNSS satellites are visible.
literature.source_title: NTN-based 6G Localization: Vision, Role of LEOs, and Open Problems

literature.comparison_caveat: LEO-augmented GNSS, not standalone cooperative NTN JCLS.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: GNSS PPP infrastructure plus simulated/augmented LEO observations.
literature.label: leo_pnt_literature_reference
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: Reports LEO-augmented GNSS PPP geometry/accuracy improvements under the paper's simulation and data assumptions.
literature.source_title: LEO enhanced GNSS precise point positioning with emphasis on model comparison

literature.comparison_caveat: The sweep values are scenario assumptions, not final literature-calibrated device models.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: Local oscillator holdover after GNSS loss.
literature.label: intermittent_gnss_update
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: TCXO frequency stability options span roughly +/-0.05 to +/-1.5 ppm.
literature.source_title: Rakon TCXO product family

literature.comparison_caveat: Application-specific timing holdover, not UE-grade clock-drift model.
literature.infrastructure_assumptions: High-quality oscillator holdover and GPS timing application.
literature.label: intermittent_gnss_update
literature.reported_or_relevant_value: Reports maintaining timing within 1 us drift for more than 1.5 hours in a GPS holdover context.
literature.source_title: PMU holdover performance enhancement using double-oven controlled oscillator

next_recommended_action: Human review should choose at most one GNSS-aided diagnostic panel plus one taxonomy/context table before any manuscript edits.
recommended_baselines_for_final_figures: Use degraded_gnss_reference as a scenario/reference panel only, with nominal/degraded/poor/unavailable levels marked as assumptions.
Use gnss_aided_initialization as a prior-sensitivity diagnostic if reviewer framing needs to answer whether a brief GNSS fix removes the need for JCLS.
Use gnss_clock_aided_ntn only as a clock-aiding/oracle diagnostic; keep perfect clock visibly labeled as oracle.
Use intermittent_gnss_update to explain why update interval and oscillator drift can dominate range bias during GNSS outages.
safe_claims: JCLS targets operating assumptions where continuous external GNSS PNT may be unavailable, intermittent, or used only as aiding.
Standalone GNSS and correction-service GNSS assume external timing/positioning infrastructure that is not infrastructure-matched to JCLS.
Perfect-clock and correction-service references are useful diagnostics or context, not fair GNSS-denied baselines.
The 0.5 ppm over 15 s example corresponds to 7.5 microseconds and about 2.25 km range-equivalent bias.

taxonomy.assumption_summary: Open-sky or otherwise usable GNSS access; no NTN cooperation needed.
taxonomy.label: standalone_gnss_reference
taxonomy.safe_claim: Useful as a familiar external PNT reference when GNSS is available.
taxonomy.satellite_clock_correction_assumed: GNSS broadcast orbit/clock correction and synchronized GNSS time.
taxonomy.ue_clock_correction_assumed: Receiver clock bias solved as part of the GNSS fix.
taxonomy.unsafe_claim: Do not claim it is an infrastructure-matched GNSS-denied baseline for JCLS.

taxonomy.assumption_summary: Nominal, degraded 10 m, poor 100 m, and unavailable scenario levels.
taxonomy.label: degraded_gnss_reference
taxonomy.safe_claim: Shows sensitivity to GNSS blockage or weak geometry as scenario assumptions.
taxonomy.satellite_clock_correction_assumed: Standard GNSS timing and broadcast corrections remain assumed.
taxonomy.ue_clock_correction_assumed: Receiver clock solved from degraded pseudorange geometry.
taxonomy.unsafe_claim: Do not present the 10 m or 100 m levels as literature values without a citation.

taxonomy.assumption_summary: One-shot GNSS position prior with NTN/JCLS refinement after GNSS loss.
taxonomy.label: gnss_aided_initialization
taxonomy.safe_claim: Tests whether good external initialization removes the need for cooperative refinement.
taxonomy.satellite_clock_correction_assumed: Only the initial GNSS fix inherits GNSS satellite timing assumptions.
taxonomy.ue_clock_correction_assumed: Initial UE position prior is aided; clock aiding is not continuous.
taxonomy.unsafe_claim: Do not call this standalone JCLS because it injects external GNSS information.

taxonomy.assumption_summary: Perfect clock, 1 ns, 10 ns, 100 ns, 1 us, and unconstrained cases.
taxonomy.label: gnss_clock_aided_ntn
taxonomy.safe_claim: Shows how much external clock synchronization changes NTN localization.
taxonomy.satellite_clock_correction_assumed: Known or tightly constrained satellite/network clocks.
taxonomy.ue_clock_correction_assumed: Known, tightly constrained, or periodically updated UE clocks.
taxonomy.unsafe_claim: Do not use the perfect-clock row as a fair synchronization baseline.

taxonomy.assumption_summary: GNSS update intervals 1, 5, 15, and 30 s with ppm/ppb drift scenarios.
taxonomy.label: intermittent_gnss_update
taxonomy.safe_claim: Quantifies holdover range bias between GNSS reacquisitions.
taxonomy.satellite_clock_correction_assumed: Clock is corrected only at GNSS update epochs.
taxonomy.ue_clock_correction_assumed: Clock uncertainty grows between updates according to oscillator drift.
taxonomy.unsafe_claim: Do not call the outage interval GNSS-free without accounting for prior GNSS information.

taxonomy.assumption_summary: RTK, PPP, DGNSS, SBAS, or commercial correction-service infrastructure.
taxonomy.label: gnss_correction_service_reference
taxonomy.safe_claim: Provides high-performance GNSS context when corrections are available.
taxonomy.satellite_clock_correction_assumed: Precise orbit/clock corrections from an augmentation service.
taxonomy.ue_clock_correction_assumed: Receiver clock solved with augmented GNSS measurements.
taxonomy.unsafe_claim: Do not compare it as a fair GNSS-denied or infrastructure-matched JCLS baseline.

taxonomy.assumption_summary: Signals, batching time, reference receiver, and orbit knowledge vary by paper.
taxonomy.label: leo_pnt_literature_reference
taxonomy.safe_claim: Useful as context for LEO-PNT and Starlink signal-of-opportunity work.
taxonomy.satellite_clock_correction_assumed: Often assumes known or estimated LEO orbit/clock information.
taxonomy.ue_clock_correction_assumed: Estimated or externally aided depending on the paper.
taxonomy.unsafe_claim: Do not claim direct superiority or inferiority without reproducing the paper assumptions.

unsafe_claims: The 10 m and 100 m degraded GNSS levels are literature values.
RTK/PPP is a fair baseline for GNSS-denied JCLS without matching correction infrastructure.
A GNSS-aided initialization row represents standalone JCLS performance.
Perfect clock oracle rows represent achievable GNSS clock aid for all UEs and NTN satellites.
